prompt: Ultra-luxury architectural photography, golden hour, Jumeirah Bay Island private residence, Dubai. Shot on Phase One IQ4 150MP camera, 40mm Schneider Kreuznach lens, f/11, ISO 200, tripod, 1/100 second exposure. Camera on the private beach, 1 meter above the sand, 20 meters from the villa, perfectly centred. Time is 6:24pm, sun 8 degrees above the western horizon directly behind camera, casting near-horizontal warm amber raking light across the entire facade. The Dubai Marina skyline is clearly visible 2.8km across the bay behind the villa — 14 towers in graduated silhouette, the tallest three catching direct sunlight on their upper two-thirds as warm gold verticals against the coral sky. The architecture is a monumental sculptural composition of two intersecting rectangular volumes deliberately set at 11 degrees to one another in plan — the angle creates a V-shaped outdoor living zone that opens directly toward the sea and the skyline beyond. The primary volume is two storeys: clad entirely in hand-raked black basalt stone laid in 300mm horizontal courses, each course projecting 12mm further than the one above it — when the near-horizontal sun rakes across, every course catches light on its projecting lip, turning the entire black facade into a ladder of amber horizontal stripes. This volume projects 5 meters forward over a ground-level open pavilion on eight polished satin-steel columns, each column 400mm square, creating a dramatic piloted entry undercroft. The ground-level open pavilion — a 22 meter wide void between columns — contains the main living space behind full-height structural glass: a curved cream-boucle sectional sofa surrounding a sunken circular conversation pit with a geometric coffee table in blackened steel and cream onyx, a freestanding suspended steel fireplace cone hanging from the soffit on a single rod, and floor-to-ceiling shelving with backlit warm amber alabaster panels casting the room in a golden caramel wash. The secondary volume is single-storey, 40 meters wide, set at the 11-degree angle: clad in champagne-anodised aluminium panels, each one slightly concave and 4 meters tall — in the raking sunlight each panel face catches a different specular highlight, making the facade shimmer between warm gold and pale silver. These two volumes connect at first-floor level via a glazed sky bridge showing a gym interior — cable machines, a rowing ergometer, and a punching bag silhouetted against the window. The V-shaped outdoor space between the volumes: sunken circular concrete fire pit with six curved low-profile teak daybeds arranged radially, an outdoor kitchen in matte anthracite with a single suspended concrete canopy, and underfloor deck lighting in cream limestone creating upward pools of warm light. Rooftop of the primary volume: an infinity pool running the full 22-meter width — its sea-facing edge is a hairline above the parapet, visible from beach level as a thin copper-toned water plane set against the sky. A sculptural spiral staircase in weathered Corten steel climbs from the terrace to the rooftop at the right edge of the building — its shadow falling diagonally across the basalt facade. Two Washingtonia robusta palms stand at ground level on the left flank, one bismarck palm on the right — the Washingtonias backlit, their dead skirt of brown fronds catching amber rim light, live fronds at the crown side-lit in warm gold. White sand beach in the foreground is unmarked except for a single set of foot impression tracks leading from bottom-left of frame to the pool edge, partially wind-blurred. Arabian Gulf water in the foreground is copper-bronze from reflected sky, 12 to 18 distinct ripple lines catching the low sun as horizontal bands of vivid bright orange. Sky: lower quarter vivid coral-tangerine bleeding into burning amber-gold in the middle, the upper half clear deep teal with a trace of green at the gradient transition — no clouds. Dubai Marina skyline towers perfectly sharp behind the villa, the gap between the two building volumes framing the skyline centrally, Burj Khalifa visible as a dark needle at the far right edge of the frame. Monumental, cinematic, unprecedented in standard real estate photography.
negative_prompt: CGI, 3D render, illustration, rendered, simple rectangular box, single volume, flat unbroken facade, no angled volumes, no cantilevering, no columns, no pilotis, no sky bridge, no rooftop pool, traditional Arabic architecture, Islamic patterns, minarets, domes, gold exterior cladding, ornate decoration, palms on roof, empty rooms, no furniture, bare interior, no sofa, no fire pit, no outdoor furniture, no outdoor kitchen, people visible, boats in foreground, jet ski, yacht, cars, tour bus, power lines, neighbouring buildings at same level, walled compound with no view, no skyline, Marina skyline missing, no Dubai towers in background, Burj Al Arab visible, watermark, overexposed facade, blown highlights, lens flare, sun starburst, HDR glow, Instagram filter, warm preset filter, desaturated, flat sky, overcast, night time without ambient sky, blue hour, no golden light, pale sky, white sky, washed-out sky, purple sky, stock photography, tourism brochure, isolated island with no city backdrop, gradient sky overlay, artificial sky replacement
